Assembly: The assembly daCirErio1.hap1.1 is based on 40x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This assembly records heterozygous inversions on Chromosome 9 (~34.1-43 Mbp) and Chromosome 16 (~49.4-56.6 Mbp).

Assembly: The assembly ilCerVinu1.hap1.1 is based on 31x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The Z chromosome was identified through synteny analysis with Clostera curtula (GCA_905475355.2)The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 6 (33.2 - 34.9 Mbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 18 (11.2 - 12.39 Mbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly dcOxyDigy1.hap1.1 is based on 216x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. the exact order and orientation of the contigs in the repetetive sequence of chromosome 5 (73.5 Mbp - end) is unknown. Multiple heterozygous inversions were observed.

Assembly: The assembly icLocCrat2.1 is based on 35x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The following regions of this assembly are of undetermined order and orientation: Chromosome 9 region 37.4 - End Mbp, Chromosome Y.
